reasoning: John Morgan is well-known for teasing political runs without following through. In mid-May 2025, he firmly stated he wants to "wait to see what the field looks like" prior to deciding and emphasized that his high name recognition allows him to take much longer than other candidates to officially open a campaign account. While there is a legal deadline around June 12, 2025, for party registration (requiring candidates to be registered with their party 365 days before the June 2026 qualifying date), this only forces Morgan to update his voter registration, not to formally announce a campaign. Given his explicit strategy to delay formalizing his campaign until the field takes shape, and the very short window between the knowledge cutoff (June 6) and the resolution date (July 1), it is highly likely he will merely keep his options open and refrain from a formal announcement.
